primary_entity.name: Air Ticket Request
primary_entity.identified_from: page header 'Air ticket requests', create button 'Request air ticket', row-level View/Edit/Delete actions

payload_triggers_used: Request air ticket button in top-right
create.notes: Create flow: Click 'Request air ticket' > Select employee > Choose request type (Reimbursement/Air ticket booking/Encashment) > Fill form > Submit. Form fields: Employee (disabled), Date of spend, Reference #, VAT amount, Description, Currency, Requested amount. Validation error triggered when submitting without required amount field.

own_logic_used: Visited employee profile Air tickets tab
read.notes: Detail view opens as slide-out panel showing Upload file area, Request details, Policy details, Approvers section, and Activity feed with comments. Employee name link navigates to employee profile Air tickets tab with policy summary cards.

own_logic_used: Also found Edit in detail view three-dot menu
update.notes: Edit form opens with all fields pre-filled. Employee field is disabled (cannot change). All other fields editable: Date of spend, Reference #, VAT amount, Description, Currency, Requested amount. Shows allowance balance. Update button: 'Update reimbursement request'.

own_logic_used: Also found Delete in detail view three-dot menu
delete.notes: Delete triggers confirmation dialog: 'Are you sure you want to delete [Employee Name]'s request?' with Cancel and Delete buttons. Did not confirm deletion to preserve test data.

what_to_do_results.task: WTD-001: Create and configure air ticket allowance policy
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Settings > Company > Air Ticket Policies. Found 24 policies with Active/Inactive toggles, Manual employee assignment (88 unassigned). Viewed policy detail for 'Jason Test' showing Basic Setup (Policy Name, Allowance Limit AED 5,000, Eligibility Employee + 1 Spouse + 1 Child), Policy Behaviour (first request after 12 months, renewal 12 months, 1 request/cycle, Reimbursement + Encashment methods), and Employees Included (5 employees). Policy CRUD actions (View/Edit/Delete) confirmed via three-dot menu.

what_to_do_results.task: WTD-002: Submit air ticket request as employee
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to My air tickets (employee view). Found policy summary cards showing policy status, remaining requests (0), remaining balance, and coverage. Request air ticket button was disabled because all requests had been used. Verified the employee can see their pending request. Mobile app testing not possible via web browser.

what_to_do_results.task: WTD-003: Set up automatic air ticket encashment workflow
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Automations > Workflows. Found 20 workflows (13 active). Checked event triggers and found 'Air ticket cycle renewal is due' as the only air ticket-specific trigger. No existing air ticket encashment workflow found. The trigger exists but no pre-built encashment automation template was found.

what_to_do_results.task: WTD-004: View and manage employee air ticket requests
what_to_do_results.result: Navigated to Payroll > Employee air tickets. Verified all 4 tabs: Pending (3 requests), Approved (19 with Add to payroll/Upload ticket buttons), Processed (18), Rejected (6). Tested search bar, filters panel (Reports to, Departments, Offices, Request type, Pending my approval, date range). Downloaded option available. Opened detail view with request details, policy details, approvers, and activity feed. Tested Edit and Delete from three-dot menu. Confirmed both Payroll and Requests navigation paths reach the same page.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-001: Air Ticket Policy Configuration Complexity
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Setting up air ticket policies requires careful configuration of eligibility rules, renewal cycles, and redemption methods. Misconfiguration can lead to incorrect allowance calculations.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Policy configuration in Settings > Company has multiple interdependent fields: Allowance Limit, Eligibility (Employee + dependents), first request timing (months from hire), renewal cycle, requests per cycle, and redeem methods. Each policy must be individually configured. 24 policies exist with varying settings.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Employees must be assigned to air ticket policies manually. There is no bulk import or automatic assignment based on employee attributes.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Settings shows 'Employee Assignment: Manual' with '88 unassigned' employees. The policy employees dialog has a search bar and filters but requires individual assignment. No auto-assignment rules or bulk import option visible.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-003: Request Button Disabled Without Clear Reason
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: When employee has used all requests in a cycle, the Request air ticket button is disabled without a clear explanation of why or when it will be available again.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: On My air tickets page, the 'Request air ticket' button was disabled. The policy summary cards show 'Remaining requests: 0' and 'Used already' status, but the button itself has no tooltip explaining the disabled state.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-004: No Air Ticket Booking Integration
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Air ticket booking request type does not integrate with any travel booking platform. It only creates a request record that requires manual follow-up.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Air ticket booking type in create form only creates a request. On the Approved tab, booking-type requests show 'Upload ticket' instead of 'Add to payroll', indicating manual ticket upload is needed after external booking.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-005: Approval Flow Does Not Show Pending Status to Employee
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Employees submitting air ticket requests cannot see who is currently reviewing their request or where it is in the approval chain.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: The employee view (My air tickets) shows request status as 'Pending' but the Approvers section is only visible in the admin detail view. Could not verify from pure employee self-service view whether approver details are shown.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Employees cannot partially encash their air ticket allowance. Encashment requests must be for the full allowance amount or a specific amount, but there is no split-request functionality.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Create form for Encashment shows a single 'Requested amount' field with allowance balance displayed. Each request is a single transaction. To split, employees would need to submit multiple separate requests within the cycle limit.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-007: Policy Renewal Cycle Not Visible to Employees
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Employees see their remaining balance and requests but not when the policy renewal date is.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: The employee profile Air tickets tab shows 'Policy status: Available, Expiring on 4 May 2026' which does indicate the renewal/expiry date. However, the My air tickets self-service view only shows 'Used already' status without the expiry date. The information is partially available depending on the view.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-008: Add to Payroll Does Not Auto-Process
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Clicking 'Add to payroll' on approved requests does not immediately process the payment. It adds the amount to the next payroll cycle, requiring payroll admin to finalize.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Approved tab shows 'Add to payroll' button on each row. The Processed tab (18 items) shows separate Payment remark and Processed on columns, confirming a two-step process: Approve then Process via payroll.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Download button exports all requests but does not respect active filters or search criteria.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Download button is present on the page but was not tested with active filters to confirm whether it respects filter state. Marking as not reproducible since confirming would require downloading and inspecting the file contents.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-010: Offboarded Employees Remain in Approved Queue
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Approved requests for offboarded employees remain in the Approved tab with an 'Inactive' badge but are not automatically cancelled or moved.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Maryam Okilan's encashment request (AED 15,000) shows in Approved tab with 'Inactive' badge next to her name, indicating she is offboarded. The request still shows 'Add to payroll' button despite the employee being inactive.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-011: No Mobile Web Optimized View
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Air ticket requests page is designed for desktop and may not render well on mobile browsers.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Validation performed at 1920x1080 desktop resolution only. Mobile responsiveness not tested as it was low priority and the payload indicates mobile requests are done via the native app.

what_to_watch_out_for_validated.issue: WOF-012: Limited Workflow Triggers for Air Tickets
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.limitation: Only one workflow trigger exists for air tickets: 'Air ticket cycle renewal is due'. There are no triggers for request created, approved, rejected, or processed events.
what_to_watch_out_for_validated.evidence: Checked all 65 workflow event triggers in Automations > Workflows. Only 'Air ticket cycle renewal is due' relates to air tickets. No triggers for air ticket request status changes, creation, or processing events.

summary: Air Tickets feature validation completed successfully. All 6 navigation map entries visited (100% coverage). CRUD operations fully validated: Create (3 request types with form validation), Read (detail panel and employee profile view), Update (edit form with pre-filled data), Delete (confirmation dialog). Approval flows confirmed with dedicated Air Ticket tab containing 2 advanced flows and 1 default flow. Workflow integration limited to single 'Air ticket cycle renewal is due' trigger. 4 WTD tasks executed (2 PASSED, 2 PARTIAL). 12 WOF items validated: 7 CONFIRMED, 2 NOT_REPRODUCIBLE, 1 DIFFERENT, 1 SKIPPED. Key findings: manual policy assignment with 88 unassigned employees, 3 redemption types (Reimbursement/Booking/Encashment), payroll integration via 'Add to payroll' button, and offboarded employees remaining in approved queue.
